entries.statement: Knowledge of program management frameworks (PMI, SAFe, Agile) and how they are adapted for data and AI programs that involve technical uncertainty, iterative development, and cross-functional dependencies.
Knowledge of data and AI program risk types, including technical risk, data availability risk, model performance risk, adoption risk, and regulatory compliance risk.
Knowledge of change management frameworks (Prosci ADKAR, Kotter) and how to adapt them for data and AI capability deployments with heterogeneous user populations.
Knowledge of adult learning design principles, including learning objectives, instructional design methods, knowledge retention research, and blended learning delivery models.
Knowledge of data literacy concepts, including reading and interpreting charts, understanding data sources, recognizing misleading visualizations, and applying basic statistical reasoning.

Knowledge of DataOps principles and practices, including pipeline monitoring approaches, automated alerting, SLA management, incident response workflows, and operational metrics for measuring data pipeline health and reliability.

Knowledge of IT service management principles as applied to data services, including service catalog design, service level management, incident and problem management, and continual service improvement.

Knowledge of technology vendor evaluation methodologies for data and AI platforms, including RFP and RFI processes, proof-of-concept design, total cost of ownership analysis, and vendor risk assessment.

Knowledge of data and AI operational budgeting practices, including cost allocation models, chargeback and showback frameworks, cloud cost optimization strategies, and ROI tracking for infrastructure investments.
Knowledge of self-service data platform principles, including data marketplace design, self-service analytics enablement, data product discovery mechanisms, access provisioning, and usage monitoring.

Knowledge of professional development and technical currency practices for data and AI practitioners, including continuing education approaches, conference and community engagement, certification pathways, skills assessment methods, and strategies for maintaining relevance as tools, methods, and best practices evolve.

Knowledge of machine learning operations (MLOps) processes and practices, including model deployment pipelines, model versioning, model monitoring and drift detection, model retraining workflows, and integration with DevOps and DataOps practices.

Knowledge of AI testing, evaluation, and validation frameworks, including AI test and evaluation methodologies, model validation approaches, robustness and adversarial testing, and verification procedures for ensuring AI system reliability and performance.

Knowledge of AI security risks, vulnerabilities, and threat vectors, including common attack patterns specific to AI systems, adversarial techniques, model poisoning, data poisoning, and security governance mechanisms for AI deployments.

Knowledge of AI ethics frameworks and responsible AI operations, including fairness assessment and bias mitigation techniques, transparency and explainability principles, accountability structures, and ethical governance mechanisms for AI systems.

Knowledge of data architecture and data services implementation approaches, including design patterns for data architectures, distributed data systems, data platform technical design, and data service integration strategies.

Knowledge of data acquisition, collection, and curation best practices, including data collection methodologies, data source evaluation, data curation and preparation processes, quality assessment approaches, and risk identification in data pipelines.

Knowledge of emerging AI technologies and trends, including monitoring evolving AI capabilities, assessing organizational readiness and applicability, evaluating emerging tools and techniques, and planning adoption strategies for new AI modalities and approaches.

Knowledge of operational performance measurement and continuous improvement methodologies for data and AI programs, including defining operational metrics, process improvement techniques, data-driven optimization approaches, and performance assessment frameworks.

Knowledge of data governance frameworks and policy implementation, including data governance operating models, data stewardship structures, policy development and administration, standardization procedures, and compliance monitoring mechanisms.

Knowledge of data quality and integrity assurance practices, including data quality frameworks and metrics, integrity validation approaches, anomaly detection and remediation, data cleansing procedures, and de-identification techniques.
